purpose: Local source of truth for CS2 Workbench inspection; do not re-query Valve pages for routine A/B/C checks.
formal_abc_control: A, B, and C must use the same Custom Paint Job finish so the ablation changes only the design route.
cs_deployment_asset_rule: Formal CS TGA files must be baked through the matching official Workbench weapon OBJ/UV Sheet AssetSpec; mesh-derived atlases for other model versions are not deployment substitutes.

A.notes: Dense crop-tolerant tileable template. Hydrographic or Spray-Paint may be used only as an extra pattern-native showcase, never as the formal A/B/C result.

B.notes: Weapon-space composition baked into the weapon's original UV atlas. Keep scale, offset, rotation, and randomization neutral.

C.notes: The selected B candidate plus one bounded render-conditioned correction or rollback. Use the same Workbench settings as B.

C+.notes: Optional Patina plus Custom Paint material enhancement. Excluded from A/B/C ablation statistics.

optional_route_a_pattern_native_preview.reporting_rule: Label as an additional showcase only; do not compare its metrics directly with formal B or C.
